---
title: "Cameron & Trivedi — Ch. 2: Data management and graphics (via StatEcon)"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

Translation of *Microeconometrics Using Stata* Chapter 2, routed through
[`StatEcon`](https://github.com/ElvisCasco/StatEcon): the Stata `summarize`
and `kdensity` commands are single calls to `stata_summarize` / `kdensity`
from the package, while data manipulation and graphs stay in plain Julia.

[Microeconometrics with R](https://ycroissant.github.io/micsr_book/)
· [UCLA Stata modules](https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/stata/modules/)
· [Github](https://github.com/thousandoaks?tab=repositories)

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon                # stata_summarize, kdensity, stata_regress, ...
using DataFrames
using ReadStatTables
using CSV
using Downloads
using Statistics, StatsBase
using FixedEffectModels
using LinearAlgebra
using KernelDensity, Loess
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")

# Tiny helper: unwrap a ReadStatTables.LabeledValue -> its underlying number.
_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x
```

# 2 Data management and graphics

## 2.1 Introduction

## 2.2 Types of data

### 2.2.1 Text or ASCII data

### 2.2.2 Internal numeric data

### 2.2.3 String data

### 2.2.4 Formats for displaying numeric data

## 2.3 Inputting data

### 2.3.1 General principles

```{julia}
#=
* Remove current dataset from memory
clear
=#
# Remove current dataset from memory (in Julia, just reassign or let GC handle it)
# Nothing needed; Julia garbage collects unused DataFrames
```

```{julia}
#=
* Set memory to 100 mb
*set memory 100m
=#
# Set memory -- not needed in Julia (automatic memory management)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Save data as a Stata dataset
*save mydata.dta, replace
=#
# Save data as a Stata dataset -- not directly applicable
# Use CSV.write("mydata.csv", df) or similar
```

```{julia}
#=
sysuse auto.dta
* Quick check that data are read in correctly
list in 1/5 // list the first five observations
=#
# sysuse auto.dta equivalent
auto = dataset("auto")
# Quick check - list first 5 observations
first(auto, 5)
```

```{julia}
#=
* describe the variables
describe 
=#
# describe the variables
DataFrames.describe(auto)
```

```{julia}
#=
summarize 
* descriptive statistics for the variables
=#
# descriptive statistics for the variables
DataFrames.describe(auto, :mean, :std, :min, :max, :nmissing)
```

```{julia}
stata_summarize(auto)
```

### 2.3.2 Inputting data already in Stata format

```{julia}
#=
* Read in existing Stata dataset
use "mus2/mus202psid92m.dta", clear
=#
# Read in existing Stata dataset
psid = dataset("mus202psid92m")
```

```{julia}
#=
* Read i n dataset from an Internet web site
use http://www.stata-press.com/data/r10/census.dta, clear
=#
# Read in dataset from an Internet web site.
# Cache locally to skip the download if `census.dta` already exists, and
# fall back gracefully when offline (the rest of this chapter doesn't
# depend on `census`, so skipping is safe).
const _CENSUS_URL  = "https://www.stata-press.com/data/r10/census.dta"
const _CENSUS_FILE = "census.dta"
census = try
    if !isfile(_CENSUS_FILE)
        Downloads.download(_CENSUS_URL, _CENSUS_FILE)
    end
    DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat(_CENSUS_FILE))
catch err
    @warn "Could not load $_CENSUS_FILE (offline or host unreachable). " *
          "Skipping this chunk; downstream chapters do not depend on it." err
    nothing
end
```

```{julia}
#=
clear
=#
# clear -- just reassign
```

```{julia}
#=
* Data input froo keyboard
input str20 name age female income
    "Barry" 25 0 4 0 . 9 9 0
    "Carrie" 30 1 37.000
    "Gary" 31 0 48. 000
end
=#
# Data input from keyboard
df_input = DataFrames.DataFrame(
    name   = ["Barry", "Carrie", "Gary"],
    age    = [25, 30, 31],
    female = [0, 1, 0],
    income = [40990.0, 37000.0, 48000.0]
)
```

```{julia}
#=
list, clean
=#
# list, clean
println(df_input)
```

### 2.3.3 Inputting data from the keyboard

### 2.3.4 Inputting nontext data

### 2.3.5 Inputting text data from a spreadsheet

```{julia}
#=
* Read data from a csv file that includes variable names using insheet
clear
insheet using "mus2/mus202file1.csv"
=#
# Read data from a csv file that includes variable names
df_csv1 = dataset("mus202file1.csv")
```

```{julia}
#=
list, clean
=#
println(df_csv1)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Read data from a csv file without variable names and assign names
clear
insheet name age female income using mus2/mus202file2.csv
=#
# Read data from a csv file without variable names and assign names
df_csv2 = dataset("mus202file2.csv"; header=[:name, :age, :female, :income])
```

### 2.3.6 Inputting text data 1n free format

```{julia}
#=
* Read data from free-format text file using infile
clear
infile str20 name age female income using mus2/mus202file2.csv
list, clean
=#
# Read data from free-format text file
df_csv2b = dataset("mus202file2.csv"; header=[:name, :age, :female, :income])
println(df_csv2b)
```

### 2.3.7 Inputting text data in fixed format

```{julia}
#=
* Read data from fixed-format text file using infix
clear
infix str20 name 1-10 age 11-12 female 13 income 14-20 using mus2/mus202file3.txt
list, clean
=#
# Read data from fixed-format text file using custom parsing
lines = readlines(datapath("mus202file3.txt"))
df_fixed = DataFrames.DataFrame(
    name   = [strip(l[1:min(10,length(l))]) for l in lines],
    age    = [parse(Int, strip(l[11:min(12,length(l))])) for l in lines],
    female = [parse(Int, strip(l[13:min(13,length(l))])) for l in lines],
    income = [parse(Float64, strip(l[14:min(20,length(l))])) for l in lines]
)
println(df_fixed)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Read data using infix where an observation spans more than one line
clear
infix str20 name 1-10 age 11-12 female 13 2: income 1-7 using mus2/mus202file4.txt
=#
# Read data where an observation spans more than one line
# Custom parsing needed for multi-line observations
# lines = readlines(datapath("mus202file4.txt"))
# Parse pairs of lines as single observations
```

### 2.3.8 Dictionary files

```{julia}
#=
* Read in data with dictionary file
*infile using mus02dict
=#
# Read in data with dictionary file -- not directly applicable in Julia
# Use CSV.read or custom parsing instead
```

### 2.3.9 Common pitfalls

## 2.4 Data management

### 2.4.1 PSiD example

```{julia}
#=
* mus02psid92m.do
clear
#delimit ;
*  PSID DATA CENTER *****************************************************
   JOBID            : 10654                             
   DATA_DOMAIN      : PSID                              
   USER_WHERE       : ER32000=1 and ER30736 ge 30 and ER
   FILE_TYPE        : All Individuals Data              
   OUTPUT_DATA_TYPE : ASCII Data File                   
   STATEMENTS       : STATA Statements                  
   CODEBOOK_TYPE    : PDF                               
   N_OF_VARIABLES   : 12                                
   N_OF_OBSERVATIONS: 4290                              
   MAX_REC_LENGTH   : 56                                
   DATE & TIME      : November 3, 2003 @ 0:28:35
*************************************************************************
;
insheet 
   ER30001 ER30002 ER32000 ER32022 ER32049 ER30733 ER30734 ER30735 ER30736
    ER30748 ER30750 ER30754
using musr/mus02psid92m.txt, delim("^") clear 
;
destring, replace ;
label variable er30001  "1968 INTERVIEW NUMBER"  ;
label variable er30002  "PERSON NUMBER                 68"  ;
label variable er32000  "SEX OF INDIVIDUAL"  ;
label variable er32022  "# LIVE BIRTHS TO THIS INDIVIDUAL"  ;
label variable er32049  "LAST KNOWN MARITAL STATUS"  ;
label variable er30733  "1992 INTERVIEW NUMBER"  ;
label variable er30734  "SEQUENCE NUMBER               92"  ;
label variable er30735  "RELATION TO HEAD              92"  ;
label variable er30736  "AGE OF INDIVIDUAL             92"  ;
label variable er30748  "COMPLETED EDUCATION           92"  ;
label variable er30750  "TOT LABOR INCOME              92"  ;
label variable er30754  "ANN WORK HRS                  92"  ;

#delimit cr;    //  Change delimiter to default cr
=#
# mus02psid92m.do equivalent - Read the PSID data
psid_raw = dataset("mus02psid92m.txt"; delim='^',
    header=[:er30001, :er30002, :er32000, :er32022, :er32049, :er30733,
            :er30734, :er30735, :er30736, :er30748, :er30750, :er30754])

# Variable labels (as comments for reference):
# er30001 = "1968 INTERVIEW NUMBER"
# er30002 = "PERSON NUMBER 68"
# er32000 = "SEX OF INDIVIDUAL"
# er32022 = "# LIVE BIRTHS TO THIS INDIVIDUAL"
# er32049 = "LAST KNOWN MARITAL STATUS"
# er30733 = "1992 INTERVIEW NUMBER"
# er30734 = "SEQUENCE NUMBER 92"
# er30735 = "RELATION TO HEAD 92"
# er30736 = "AGE OF INDIVIDUAL 92"
# er30748 = "COMPLETED EDUCATION 92"
# er30750 = "TOT LABOR INCOME 92"
# er30754 = "ANN WORK HRS 92"
```

```{julia}
#=
* Data description
describe
=#
# Data description
DataFrames.describe(psid_raw)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Data summary
summarize
=#
# Data summary
DataFrames.describe(psid_raw, :mean, :std, :min, :max, :nmissing)
```

### 2.4.2 Naming and labeling variables

```{julia}
#=
* Rename variables
rename er32000 sex
rename er30736 age
rename er30748 education
rename er30750 earnings
rename er30754 hours
=#
# Rename variables
psid = copy(psid_raw)
rename!(psid,
    :er32000 => :sex,
    :er30736 => :age,
    :er30748 => :education,
    :er30750 => :earnings,
    :er30754 => :hours
)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Relabel some of the variables
label variable age "AGE OF INDIVIDUAL"
label variable education "COMPLETED EDUCATION"
label variable earnings "TOT LABOR INCOME"
label variable hours "ANN WORK HRS"
=#
# Relabel -- Julia DataFrames don't have built-in labels
# We use a dictionary for reference
labels = Dict(
    :age       => "AGE OF INDIVIDUAL",
    :education => "COMPLETED EDUCATION",
    :earnings  => "TOT LABOR INCOME",
    :hours     => "ANN WORK HRS"
)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Define the label gender for the values taken by variable sex
label define gender 1 male 2 female
label values sex gender
list sex in 1/2, clean
=#
# Define the label gender for the values taken by variable sex
sex_labels = Dict(1 => "male", 2 => "female")
println("First 2 observations of sex:")
for i in 1:2
    println("  $(i): $(get(sex_labels, psid.sex[i], "unknown"))")
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Data summary of key variables after renaming
summarize sex age education earnings hours
=#
stata_summarize(psid, [:sex, :age, :education, :earnings, :hours])
```

### 2.4.3 Viewing data

```{julia}
#=
* List first 2 observations of two of the variables
list age hours in 1/2, clean
=#
# List first 2 observations of two of the variables
println(psid[1:2, [:age, :hours]])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Tabulate all values taken by a single variable
tabulate education
=#
# Tabulate all values taken by a single variable
edu_tab = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(psid, :education), nrow => :Freq)
sort!(edu_tab, :education)
println(edu_tab)
```

### 2.4.4 Using original documentation

### 2.4.5 Missing values

```{julia}
#=
* Replace missing values with missing-data code
replace education = . if education == 0 | education == 99
=#
# Replace missing values with missing-data code
psid.education = convert(Vector{Union{Missing, eltype(psid.education)}}, psid.education)
psid.education[coalesce.((psid.education .== 0) .| (psid.education .== 99), false)] .= missing
```

```{julia}
#=
* Listing of variable including missing value
list education in 46/48, clean
=#
# Listing of variable including missing value
println(psid[46:48, [:education]])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Example of data analysis with some missing values
summarize education age
=#
stata_summarize(psid, [:education, :age])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Replace missing values with missing-data code
replace earnings = . if education >= .
replace hours = . if education >= .
=#
# Replace earnings and hours to missing where education is missing
psid.earnings = convert(Vector{Union{Missing, eltype(psid.earnings)}}, psid.earnings)
psid.hours    = convert(Vector{Union{Missing, eltype(psid.hours)}}, psid.hours)
psid.earnings[ismissing.(psid.education)] .= missing
psid.hours[ismissing.(psid.education)]    .= missing
```

```{julia}
#=
* Assign more than one missing code
replace education = .a if education == 0
replace education = .b if education == 99
=#
# Assign more than one missing code
# Julia has a single `missing` type; use separate flag columns if needed
# psid.education_miss_a = psid_raw.er30748 .== 0
# psid.education_miss_b = psid_raw.er30748 .== 99
```

```{julia}
#=
* This command will include missing values
list education in 40/60 if education > 16, clean
=#
# This command will include missing values
sub_gt16 = psid[40:60, :]
sub_gt16 = sub_gt16[coalesce.(sub_gt16.education .> 16, true), [:education]]
println(sub_gt16)
```

```{julia}
#=
* This command will not include missing values
list education in 40/60 if education > 16 & education < . , clean
=#
# This command will not include missing values
sub_gt16_nomiss = psid[40:60, :]
sub_gt16_nomiss = sub_gt16_nomiss[
    coalesce.(sub_gt16_nomiss.education .> 16, false) .& .!ismissing.(sub_gt16_nomiss.education),
    [:education]]
println(sub_gt16_nomiss)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Replace missing values 1o1ith missing-data code
replace earnings = . if education >= .
replace hours = . if education >= .
=#
# Replace earnings and hours to missing where education is missing
psid.earnings[ismissing.(psid.education)] .= missing
psid.hours[ismissing.(psid.education)]    .= missing
```

### 2.4.6 imputing missing data

### 2.4.7 Transforming data (generate, replace, egen, recode)

```{julia}
#=
* Summarize cleaned up data
summarize sex age education earnings
=#
stata_summarize(psid, [:sex, :age, :education, :earnings])
```

#### The generate and replace commands

```{julia}
#=
* Create identifier using generate command
generate id = _n
=#
# Create identifier using generate command
psid.id = 1:DataFrames.nrow(psid)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create new variable using generate command
generate lnearns = ln(earnings)
=#
# Create new variable using generate command
psid.lnearns = [ismissing(e) ? missing : (e > 0 ? log(e) : missing) for e in psid.earnings]
```

#### The egen command

```{julia}
#=
* Create new variable using egen command
egen aveearnings = mean(earnings) if earnings < .
=#
# Create new variable using egen command (mean of earnings)
psid.aveearnings = fill(Statistics.mean(skipmissing(psid.earnings)), DataFrames.nrow(psid))
```

#### The recode command

```{julia}
#=
* Replace existing data using the recode command
recode education (1/11=1) (12=2) (13/15=3) (16/17=4), generate(edcat) 
=#
# Replace existing data using the recode command
psid.edcat = [ismissing(e) ? missing :
              e <= 11 ? 1 :
              e == 12 ? 2 :
              e <= 15 ? 3 :
              e <= 17 ? 4 : missing
              for e in psid.education]
```

#### The by prefix

```{julia}
#=
* Create new variable using bysort: prefix
bysort education: egen aveearnsbyed = mean(earnings)
sort id
=#
# Create new variable using bysort: prefix (mean earnings by education)
psid_nomiss = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid, [:education, :earnings])
aveby = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(psid_nomiss, :education), :earnings => mean => :aveearnsbyed)
psid = DataFrames.leftjoin(psid, aveby, on=:education, makeunique=true, matchmissing=:equal)
sort!(psid, :id)
```

#### Indicator variables

```{julia}
#=
* Create indicator variable using generate command with logical operators
generate d1 = earnings > 0 if earnings < .
=#
# Create indicator variable using logical operators
psid.d1 = [ismissing(e) ? missing : (e > 0 ? 1 : 0) for e in psid.earnings]
```

```{julia}
#=
summarize d1
=#
stata_summarize(psid, :d1)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create indicator variable using generate and replace commands
generate d2 = 0
replace d2 = 1 if earnings > 0
replace d2 = . if earnings >= .
=#
# Create indicator variable using generate and replace commands
psid.d2 = zeros(Int, DataFrames.nrow(psid))
psid.d2[coalesce.(psid.earnings .> 0, false)] .= 1
psid.d2 = [ismissing(e) ? missing : psid.d2[i] for (i, e) in enumerate(psid.earnings)]
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create indicator variable using recode commands
recode earnings (0=0) (1/999999=1), generate(d3)
=#
# Create indicator variable using recode
psid.d3 = [ismissing(e) ? missing : (e > 0 ? 1 : 0) for e in psid.earnings]
```

```{julia}
#=
* Verify that three methods create the same indicator variable
// summarize d1 d2 d3
summarize d1 d2 d3
=#
stata_summarize(psid, [:d1, :d2, :d3])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create a set of indicator variables using tabulate with generate() option
quietly tabulate edcat, generate(eddummy)
summarize eddummy*
=#
# Create a set of indicator variables using tabulate with generate() option
edcat_vals = sort(unique(skipmissing(psid.edcat)))
for val in edcat_vals
    psid[!, Symbol("eddummy$val")] = [ismissing(e) ? missing : (e == val ? 1 : 0) for e in psid.edcat]
end
eddummy_cols = [Symbol("eddummy$v") for v in edcat_vals]
stata_summarize(psid, eddummy_cols)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create a set of indicator variables using command xi
xi i.edcat, noomit
summarize _I*
=#
# xi i.edcat  -- same 0/1 dummies as `eddummy*` above.
stata_summarize(psid, eddummy_cols)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Command with a variable list that includes indicators created using xi:
xi : summarize i.edcat
=#
stata_summarize(psid, eddummy_cols)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Set of indicator variables using factor variables - no category is omitted
summarize ibn.edcat
=#
# Set of indicator variables using factor variables - no category is omitted
for val in edcat_vals
    n = count(x -> x === val, psid.edcat)
    println("edcat==$val: N=$n")
end
```

#### interactions

```{julia}
#=
* Create interactive variable using generate commands
generate d1education = d1*education
=#
# Create interactive variable using generate commands
psid.d1education = [ismissing(d) || ismissing(e) ? missing : d * e for (d, e) in zip(psid.d1, psid.education)]
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create set of interactions betyeen cat variable and set of indicators
drop _Iedcat_*
xi i.edcat*earnings, noomit
=#
# Create set of interactions between categorical variable and earnings
for val in edcat_vals
    psid[!, Symbol("_Iedcat_$(val)Xearnings")] = [
        (ismissing(ec) || ismissing(ea)) ? missing : (ec == val ? 1 : 0) * ea
        for (ec, ea) in zip(psid.edcat, psid.earnings)
    ]
end
```

```{julia}
#=
summarize _I*
=#
int_cols = [Symbol("_Iedcat_$(v)Xearnings") for v in edcat_vals]
stata_summarize(psid, int_cols)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create a set of interactions between a categorical and a set of indicators
drop _I*
xi i.edcat*i.d1, noomit
=#
# Create a set of interactions between two categorical variables (edcat x d1)
for ev in edcat_vals
    for dv in [0, 1]
        colname = Symbol("_Iedcat$(ev)Xd1$(dv)")
        psid[!, colname] = [
            (ismissing(ec) || ismissing(d)) ? missing : ((ec == ev && d == dv) ? 1 : 0)
            for (ec, d) in zip(psid.edcat, psid.d1)
        ]
    end
end
```

```{julia}
#=
summarize _I*
=#
int2_cols = vec([Symbol("_Iedcat$(ev)Xd1$(dv)") for ev in edcat_vals, dv in [0, 1]])
stata_summarize(psid, int2_cols)
```

#### Demeaning

```{julia}
#=
* Create demeaned variables
egen double aveage = mean(age)
generate double agedemean = age - aveage
generate double agesqdemean = agedemean^2
summarize agedemean agesqdemean
=#
# Create demeaned variables
aveage = Statistics.mean(skipmissing(psid.age))
psid.agedemean   = psid.age .- aveage
psid.agesqdemean = psid.agedemean .^ 2
col1 = collect(skipmissing(psid.agedemean))
col2 = collect(skipmissing(psid.agesqdemean))
println("agedemean:   N=$(length(col1))  Mean=$(round(mean(col1), digits=6))  Std=$(round(std(col1), digits=4))")
println("agesqdemean: N=$(length(col2))  Mean=$(round(mean(col2), digits=4))  Std=$(round(std(col2), digits=4))")
```

### 2.4.8 Saving data

```{julia}
#=
* Save as Stata data file
save musr/mus02psid92m_test.dta, replace
=#
# Save as Stata data file -- save as CSV instead
CSV.write("mus02psid92m_test.csv", psid)
```

```{julia}
#=
* Save as comma-separated values spreadsheet
outsheet age education eddummy* earnings d1 hours using musr/mus02psid92m_test.csv, comma replace
=#
# Save as comma-separated values spreadsheet
let
    eddummy_local = Symbol[Symbol("eddummy$v") for v in edcat_vals]
    save_cols = [:age, :education]
    append!(save_cols, eddummy_local)
    append!(save_cols, [:earnings, :d1, :hours])
    CSV.write("mus02psid92m_test_subset.csv", psid[!, save_cols])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Save as formatted text (ascii) file
outfile age education eddummy* earnings d1 hours using musr/mus02psid92m.asc, replace
=#
# Save as formatted text (ascii) file
let
    eddummy_local = Symbol[Symbol("eddummy$v") for v in edcat_vals]
    cols = [:age, :education]
    append!(cols, eddummy_local)
    append!(cols, [:earnings, :d1, :hours])
    sub = psid[!, cols]
    open("mus02psid92m.asc", "w") do io
        for i in 1:DataFrames.nrow(sub)
            parts = String[]
            for c in cols
                v = sub[i, c]
                push!(parts, ismissing(v) ? "." : string(v))
            end
            println(io, join(parts, " "))
        end
    end
end
```

### 2.4.9 Selecting the sample

```{julia}
#=
* Select the sample used in a single command using the if qualifier
summarize earnings lnearns if age >= 35 & age <= 44 
=#
# Select the sample used in a single command using the if qualifier
sub_age = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid, [:earnings, :lnearns, :age])
sub_age = filter(r -> r.age >= 35 && r.age <= 44, sub_age)
stata_summarize(sub_age, [:earnings, :lnearns])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Select the sample using command keep
keep if (lnearns != .) & (age >= 35 & age <= 44) 
summarize earnings lnearns
=#
# Select the sample using keep (filter)
psid_filt = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid, [:lnearns, :age])
psid_filt = filter(r -> !ismissing(r.lnearns) && r.age >= 35 && r.age <= 44, psid_filt)
stata_summarize(psid_filt, [:earnings, :lnearns])
```

```{julia}
#=
* Select the sample using keep and drop commands
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
keep lnearns age
drop in 1/1000
=#
# Select the sample using keep and drop commands
psid_reload = dataset("mus02psid92m")
psid_sub = psid_reload[!, [:lnearns, :age]]
psid_sub = psid_sub[1001:end, :]  # drop first 1000 observations
```

## 2.5 Manipulating datasets

### 2.5.1 Ordering observations and variables

### 2.5.2 Preserving and restoring a dataset

```{julia}
#=
clear all
* Commands preserve and restore illustrated
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
list age in 1/1, noheader clean
*preserve
replace age = age + 1000
list age in 1/1, noheader clean
*restore
list age in 1/1, noheader clean
=#
# Commands preserve and restore illustrated
psid_full = dataset("mus02psid92m")
println("Original age[1]: ", psid_full.age[1])
# preserve equivalent: make a copy
psid_backup = copy(psid_full)
psid_full.age .= psid_full.age .+ 1000
println("Modified age[1]: ", psid_full.age[1])
# restore equivalent: restore from copy
psid_full = copy(psid_backup)
println("Restored age[1]: ", psid_full.age[1])
```

### 2.5.3 Wide and long forms for a dataset

### 2.5.4 Merging datasets

```{julia}
#=
* Create first dataset with every third observation
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
keep if mod(_n,3) == 0 
keep id education earnings
list in 1/4, clean
quietly save musr/merge1.dta, replace
=#
# Create first dataset with every third observation
psid_full = dataset("mus02psid92m")
merge1 = psid_full[mod.(1:DataFrames.nrow(psid_full), 3) .== 0, [:id, :education, :earnings]]
println("merge1 first 4 rows:")
println(first(merge1, 4))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Create second dataset with every second observation
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
keep if mod(_n,2) == 0 
keep id education hours
list in 1/4, clean
quietly save musr/merge2.dta, replace
=#
# Create second dataset with every second observation
merge2 = psid_full[mod.(1:DataFrames.nrow(psid_full), 2) .== 0, [:id, :education, :hours]]
println("merge2 first 4 rows:")
println(first(merge2, 4))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Merge two datasets with some observations and variables different
clear
use musr/merge1.dta
sort id
merge 1:1 id using musr/merge2.dta    
sort id
list in 1/4, clean
=#
# Merge two datasets with some observations and variables different
merged = outerjoin(merge1, merge2, on=[:id, :education], makeunique=true, matchmissing=:equal)
sort!(merged, :id)
println("Merged first 4 rows:")
println(first(merged, 4))
```

```{julia}
#=
tab _merge
=#
# Tabulate merge indicator
merged.source = ifelse.(.!ismissing.(merged.earnings) .& .!ismissing.(merged.hours), "both",
    ifelse.(.!ismissing.(merged.earnings), "merge1_only", "merge2_only"))
println(DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(merged, :source), nrow => :Freq))
```

### 2.5.5 Appending datasets

```{julia}
#=
* Append two datasets with some observations and variables different
clear
use musr/merge1.dta
append using musr/merge2.dta
sort id
list in 1/5, clean
=#
# Append two datasets with some observations and variables different
appended = vcat(merge1, merge2, cols=:union)
sort!(appended, :id)
println("Appended first 5 rows:")
println(first(appended, 5))
```

## 2.6 Graphical display of data

### 2.6.1 Stata graph commands

#### Example graph commands

```{julia}
#=
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
twoway scatter lnearns hours
graph export musr/mus02two1.eps, replace
=#
# Load data and create scatter plot
psid_plot = dataset("mus02psid92m")
psid_plot_clean = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, [:lnearns, :hours])

fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Hours", ylabel="Log Earnings")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, psid_plot_clean.hours, psid_plot_clean.lnearns, markersize=4)
CairoMakie.save("mus02two1.png", fig)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* More advanced graphics command with two plots and with several options
graph twoway (scatter lnearns hours, msize(small))     ///
   (lfit lnearns hours, lwidth(medthick)),             ///
   title("Scatterplot and OLS fitted line")       
graph export musr/mus02two2.eps, replace
=#
# More advanced graphics command with scatter and OLS fitted line
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Hours", ylabel="Log Earnings",
    title="Scatterplot and OLS fitted line")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, psid_plot_clean.hours, psid_plot_clean.lnearns, markersize=4, color=:steelblue)
# OLS fitted line
model_plot = FixedEffectModels.reg(psid_plot_clean, @formula(lnearns ~ hours))
x_range = range(minimum(psid_plot_clean.hours), maximum(psid_plot_clean.hours), length=100)
y_hat = StatsBase.coef(model_plot)[1] .+ StatsBase.coef(model_plot)[2] .* x_range
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, collect(x_range), y_hat, color=:red, linewidth=2)
CairoMakie.save("mus02two2.png", fig)
fig
```

#### Saving and exporting graphs

```{julia}
#=
* More advanced graph example 
graph twoway (scatter lnearns hours, msize(small))    ///
  (lfit lnearns hours, lwidth(medthick)),             ///
  title("Scatterplot and OLS fitted line") 
graph export musr/mus02examplefigure.eps, replace
=#
# More advanced graph example (same as above)
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Hours", ylabel="Log Earnings",
    title="Scatterplot and OLS fitted line")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, psid_plot_clean.hours, psid_plot_clean.lnearns, markersize=4, color=:steelblue)
model_plot = FixedEffectModels.reg(psid_plot_clean, @formula(lnearns ~ hours))
x_range = range(minimum(psid_plot_clean.hours), maximum(psid_plot_clean.hours), length=100)
y_hat = StatsBase.coef(model_plot)[1] .+ StatsBase.coef(model_plot)[2] .* x_range
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, collect(x_range), y_hat, color=:red, linewidth=2)
CairoMakie.save("mus02examplefigure.png", fig)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* Combine graphs saved as graph1 . gph and graph2. gph
*graph combine graph1 graph2
=#
# Combine graphs -- in CairoMakie, use subplots in a Figure
# fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
# ax1 = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], ...)
# ax2 = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 2], ...)
# CairoMakie.save("combined.png", fig)
```

#### Learning how to use graph commands

### 2.6.2 Box-and-whisker plot

```{julia}
#=
* Simple box-and-Yhisker plot
graph box hours
=#
# Simple box-and-whisker plot
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], ylabel="Hours")
boxplot!(ax, ones(Int, length(psid_plot_clean.hours)), Float64.(psid_plot_clean.hours))
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
use musr/mus02psid92m.dta, clear
label define edtype 1 "< High School" 2 "High School" 3 "Some College" 4 "College Degree"
label values edcat edtype
=#
# Prepare edcat labels
psid_plot = dataset("mus02psid92m")
psid_plot.edcat = [ismissing(e) ? missing :
    e <= 11 ? 1 : e == 12 ? 2 : e <= 15 ? 3 : e <= 17 ? 4 : missing
    for e in psid_plot.education]
edtype = Dict(1 => "< High School", 2 => "High School", 3 => "Some College", 4 => "College Degree")
```

```{julia}
#=
* Box and whisker plot of single variable over several categories
graph box hours, over(edcat) scale(1.2) marker(1,msize(vsmall))   ///
  ytitle("Annual hours worked by education") yscale(titlegap(*5)) 
quietly graph export musr/mus02boxfig.eps, replace
=#
# Box and whisker plot of single variable over several categories
psid_box = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, [:hours, :edcat])
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1],
    ylabel="Annual hours worked by education",
    xticks=(1:4, [edtype[i] for i in 1:4]))
boxplot!(ax, Int.(psid_box.edcat), Float64.(psid_box.hours), markersize=3)
CairoMakie.save("mus02boxfig.png", fig)
fig
```

### 2.6.3 Histogram

```{julia}
#=
histogram lnearns
=#
# histogram lnearns
psid_hist = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, :lnearns)
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Log Earnings", ylabel="Density")
hist!(ax, Float64.(psid_hist.lnearns), normalization=:pdf)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* Histogram with bin width and start value set
histogram lnearns, width(0.25) start(4.0)
graph export musr/mus02hist.eps, replace
=#
# Histogram with bin width and start value set
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Log Earnings", ylabel="Density")
bins = range(4.0, maximum(skipmissing(psid_hist.lnearns)) + 0.25, step=0.25)
hist!(ax, Float64.(psid_hist.lnearns), bins=collect(bins), normalization=:pdf)
CairoMakie.save("mus02hist.png", fig)
fig
```

### 2.6.4 Kernel density plot

```{julia}
#=
kdensity lnearns
=#
# kdensity lnearns
psid_kd = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, :lnearns)
kd = KernelDensity.kde(Float64.(psid_kd.lnearns))
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Log Earnings", ylabel="Density")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, kd.x, kd.density, linewidth=2)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* Kernel density plot with bandwidth set and fitted normal density overlaid
kdensity lnearns, bwidth(0.20) normal n(4000)
graph export musr/mus02kd1.eps, replace
=#
# Kernel density plot with bandwidth set and fitted normal density overlaid
kd2 = KernelDensity.kde(Float64.(psid_kd.lnearns), bandwidth=0.20, npoints=4000)
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Log Earnings", ylabel="Density")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, kd2.x, kd2.density, linewidth=2, label="Kernel density")
# Overlay normal density
mu_kd = Statistics.mean(Float64.(psid_kd.lnearns))
sigma_kd = Statistics.std(Float64.(psid_kd.lnearns))
x_norm = range(minimum(kd2.x), maximum(kd2.x), length=4000)
y_norm = @. (1 / (sigma_kd * sqrt(2pi))) * exp(-0.5 * ((x_norm - mu_kd) / sigma_kd)^2)
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, collect(x_norm), y_norm, linewidth=2, linestyle=:dash, label="Normal")
axislegend(ax)
CairoMakie.save("mus02kd1.png", fig)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* Histogram and nonparametric kernel density estimate 
histogram lnearns if lnearns > 0, width(0.25) kdensity             ///
  kdenopts(bwidth(0.2) lwidth(medthick))                               ///
  plotregion(style(none)) scale(1.2)                                   ///
  title("Histogram and density for log earnings")                      ///
  xtitle("Log annual earnings", size(medlarge)) xscale(titlegap(*5))   ///
  ytitle("Histogram and density", size(medlarge)) yscale(titlegap(*5)) 
graph export musr/mus02histdens.eps, replace
=#
# Histogram and nonparametric kernel density estimate
psid_hd = filter(r -> !ismissing(r.lnearns) && r.lnearns > 0, psid_plot)
kd3 = KernelDensity.kde(Float64.(psid_hd.lnearns), bandwidth=0.20)
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1],
    title="Histogram and density for log earnings",
    xlabel="Log annual earnings",
    ylabel="Histogram and density")
bins = range(minimum(Float64.(psid_hd.lnearns)), maximum(Float64.(psid_hd.lnearns)) + 0.25, step=0.25)
hist!(ax, Float64.(psid_hd.lnearns), bins=collect(bins), normalization=:pdf, color=(:steelblue, 0.5))
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, kd3.x, kd3.density, linewidth=2, color=:red)
CairoMakie.save("mus02histdens.png", fig)
fig
```

### 2.6.5 Twoway scatterplots and fitted lines

```{julia}
#=
* Simple two-way scatterplot
scatter lnearns hours
=#
# Simple two-way scatterplot
psid_sc = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, [:lnearns, :hours])
fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Hours", ylabel="Log Earnings")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, Float64.(psid_sc.hours), Float64.(psid_sc.lnearns), markersize=4)
fig
```

```{julia}
#=
* Two-way scatterplot and quadratic regression curve with 95% ci for y|x  
twoway (qfitci lnearns hours, stdf)  (scatter lnearns hours, msize(small))
graph export musr/mus02scatter2.eps, replace
=#
# Two-way scatterplot and quadratic regression curve with 95% ci
psid_sc.hours_f  = Float64.(psid_sc.hours)
psid_sc.lnearns_f = Float64.(psid_sc.lnearns)
psid_sc.hours2   = psid_sc.hours_f .^ 2
model_quad = FixedEffectModels.reg(psid_sc, @formula(lnearns_f ~ hours_f + hours2))

x_sorted = sort(psid_sc.hours_f)
newdata = DataFrames.DataFrame(hours_f=x_sorted, hours2=x_sorted .^ 2)
pred = FixedEffectModels.predict(model_quad, newdata)

fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="Hours", ylabel="Log Earnings")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, x_sorted, pred, color=:blue, linewidth=2)
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, psid_sc.hours_f, psid_sc.lnearns_f, markersize=4, color=:gray)
CairoMakie.save("mus02scatter2.png", fig)
fig
```

### 2.6.6 lowess, kernel, local linear, and nearest-neighbor regression

```{julia}
#=
* Scatterplot with lowess and local linear nonparametric regression
graph twoway (scatter lnearns hours, msize(tiny))                 ///
  (lowess lnearns hours, clstyle(p1) lwidth(medthick))            /// 
  (lpoly lnearns hours, kernel(epan2) degree(1) lwidth(medthick)  ///
  bwidth(500)), plotregion(style(none))                           ///
  title("Scatterplot, lowess, and local linear regression")        ///
  xtitle("Annual hours", size(medlarge))                          /// 
  ytitle("Natural logarithm of annual earnings", size(medlarge)) ///
  legend(pos(4) ring(0) col(1)) legend(size(small))              ///
  legend(label(1 "Actual Data") label(2 "Lowess") label(3 "Local linear"))
quietly graph export musr/mus02twoway.eps, replace
=#
# Scatterplot with lowess and local linear nonparametric regression
psid_np = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, [:lnearns, :hours])
psid_np = filter(r -> isfinite(r.lnearns) && isfinite(r.hours), psid_np)
sorted_idx = sortperm(Float64.(psid_np.hours))
hours_s   = Float64.(psid_np.hours[sorted_idx])
lnearns_s = Float64.(psid_np.lnearns[sorted_idx])

# Lowess — Stata default span ≈ 0.8
loess_model = Loess.loess(hours_s, lnearns_s, span=0.8)
hours_grid  = collect(range(minimum(hours_s), maximum(hours_s), length=200))
lowess_pred = Loess.predict(loess_model, hours_grid)

# Local linear with Epanechnikov kernel and fixed bandwidth = 500
# Stata: lpoly lnearns hours, kernel(epan2) degree(1) bwidth(500)
# Loess.jl only supports tricube kernel with span (fraction), so we use a
# manual local-linear estimator with the Epanechnikov kernel and bwidth=500.
# Stata's lpoly requires a minimum effective sample size (degree+1 non-zero
# weights) to produce a prediction; grid points with too few observations in
# the kernel window are set to NaN (and therefore not plotted).
function lpoly(x, y, xgrid; bw=500.0, degree=1, min_obs=max(degree+2, 5))
    n = length(x)
    ŷ = similar(xgrid)
    for (j, x0) in enumerate(xgrid)
        u = (x .- x0) ./ bw
        # Epanechnikov kernel: K(u) = 0.75(1 - u²) for |u| ≤ 1
        w = @. ifelse(abs(u) <= 1.0, 0.75 * (1.0 - u^2), 0.0)
        n_eff = count(w .> 0)      # observations inside the kernel window
        if n_eff < min_obs
            ŷ[j] = NaN             # too few points — suppress prediction
            continue
        end
        if degree == 0
            ŷ[j] = dot(w, y) / sum(w)
        else
            X_loc = hcat(ones(n), x .- x0)
            W = LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(w)
            β_loc = (X_loc' * W * X_loc) \ (X_loc' * W * y)
            ŷ[j] = β_loc[1]
        end
    end
    return ŷ
end
ll_pred = lpoly(hours_s, lnearns_s, hours_grid; bw=500.0, degree=1)

# Filter out NaN predictions so lines stop where data is too sparse
# (matches Stata's behaviour of not drawing lpoly in empty regions)
valid_ll = .!isnan.(ll_pred)

fig = CairoMakie.Figure()
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[1, 1],
    title="Scatterplot, lowess, and local linear regression",
    xlabel="Annual hours",
    ylabel="Natural logarithm of annual earnings")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, hours_s, lnearns_s, markersize=2, color=(:gray, 0.5), label="Actual Data")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, hours_grid, lowess_pred, linewidth=2, color=:blue, label="Lowess")
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, hours_grid[valid_ll], ll_pred[valid_ll],
    linewidth=2, color=:red, label="Local linear")
axislegend(ax, position=:rc)
CairoMakie.save("mus02twoway.png", fig)
fig
```

### 2.6.7 Multiple scatterplots

```{julia}
#=
* Multiple scatterplots
label variable age "Age"
label variable lnearns "Log earnings"
label variable hours "Annual hours"
graph matrix lnearns hours age, by(edcat) msize(small) 
=#
# Multiple scatterplots (scatter matrix by edcat)
psid_mat = DataFrames.dropmissing(psid_plot, [:lnearns, :hours, :age, :edcat])
edcat_vals_plot = sort(unique(psid_mat.edcat))
vars = [:lnearns, :hours, :age]
var_labels = ["Log earnings", "Annual hours", "Age"]
nv = length(vars)
nc = length(edcat_vals_plot)

fig = CairoMakie.Figure(size=(300 * nc, 300 * nv))
for (ci, ec) in enumerate(edcat_vals_plot)
    sub = filter(r -> r.edcat == ec, psid_mat)
    for (ri, yv) in enumerate(vars)
        for (cj, xv) in enumerate(vars)
            ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig[ri, cj + (ci-1)*nv],
                xlabel=(ri == nv ? var_labels[cj] : ""),
                ylabel=(cj == 1 ? var_labels[ri] : ""),
                title=(ri == 1 ? "edcat=$(edtype[ec])" : ""))
            CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, Float64.(sub[!, xv]), Float64.(sub[!, yv]), markersize=3)
        end
    end
end
fig
```

## 2.7 Stata resources

## 2.8 Exercises
